fixture_note: Static Day-2 fixture. Two record families: (1) LIVE 8-hour seat log under `events`; (2) PRIOR-WINDOW co-seating snapshots under `co_seating_history` (every record tagged window:"history"), kept strictly separate from the live log. Live `events`: event_type is join | leave | seat_hold (FairPlay hold-pending-review, no physical seating). Case-player events are fully detailed and unchanged. Background players seated in the hour-0 room carry one pre-sim join (hour -1). Players not in the hour-0 room appear via BACKGROUND LIVE COVERAGE so all 120 players have a live event: coverage:"background_replacement" = a persistent join realizing an arrival room_state_hourly.json narrates (T-14 hour-4 replacement); coverage:"background_transient" = a within-hour join+leave at a genuinely-open seat that nets to zero at end-of-hour, changing NO seated_count in room_state_hourly.json. NOTE: P-108/P-109 are legacy gap-player IDs (deliberately absent from players.json) that retain pre-sim events SE-0020/SE-0029/SE-0130/SE-0131 -- treat as fixture artifacts, NOT valid roster members. P2b fix this batch: SE-0040 retargeted P-217->P-216 (see its fix_note) so T-15 reconciles under both occupancy semantics. Room-change events document the hourly transitions in room_state_hourly.json. `co_seating_history` (NEW Contract-1 key for consumer P3): a terse prior-window list of shared/solo recent table sittings for the integrity case clusters/households. window:"history" marks these HISTORICAL -- NOT live seat events; must never enter occupancy reconciliation. Count records where shared==true over total to re-derive the co-seating rates in relationships.json: CL-001 (P-198/199/200) = 14/18 (0.778); household H-01 (P-192/P-193) = 2/22 (0.091). These rates are historical and intentionally cannot fit inside the 8-hour live window (players sit ~100 min/table), which is why they live here, not in `events`.

events.fix_note: P2b correction: was P-217 (already seated since hour -1 via SE-0186, a no-op duplicate join). Retargeted to P-216, who left at hour 5 (SE-0032) and returns from break at hour 8 per room_state_hourly narrative -- makes T-15 reconcile under both set and counter occupancy semantics.
